results.description: Cargo.toml exists at root
src/lib.rs or src/main.rs exists
src/ directory exists
No nested src/ dirs (src/src/)
benches/ directory exists (if benchmarks declared)
package.name exists
package.version exists
package.edition exists
package.description exists
package.license or package.license-file exists
package.repository exists
package.authors exists
package.rust-version exists
package.keywords exists (libraries)
package.categories exists (libraries)
[lib] path matches existing file
[[bin]] path matches existing file
[[test]] targets declared for tests/ files
[[bench]] targets have harness = false
No undeclared test files
No undeclared bench files
[[example]] targets exist if examples/ present
[[test]] paths resolve to existing files
Package name uses kebab-case
Source files use snake_case naming
No uppercase in source file names
Module file names match mod declarations
Binary names use hyphens or underscores
Test files use correct suffixes (_test.rs, _int_test.rs, _e2e_test.rs, etc.)

Test function name prefix follows location-aware convention: <feature>_<type_kind>_<fn>_<suffix> for tests/<file>.rs; <type_kind>_<fn>_<suffix> for tests/<feature>/<file>.rs (type_kind ∈ {trait,struct,type,enum})

Test function name uses either the conventional test_ prefix or a recognised test-category suffix (_test, _int_test, _e2e_test, _perf_test, _security_test, _stress_test, _load_test, _unit_test, _smoke_test, _contract_test)
Colocated #[cfg(test)] blocks only permitted when testing private methods
No test code in src/ (non-#[cfg(test)])
doc/ or docs/ directory exists (if library)
examples/ directory exists (if library)
No generated files committed (target/)
api/ layer must not import from core/ (L2 must not depend on L3)

violations.message: File contains forbidden pattern '^\s*use crate::core'
violations.fix_hint: Remove any core:: import from api/; define abstractions in api/traits.rs and inject via trait objects

results.description: saf/ must not re-export from core/ (SAF re-exports from api/ and spi/ only)
Core modules must implement their api traits (impl <Trait> for)
L2 items (struct, enum, type, trait, fn, const, static) must use pub(crate) visibility, not bare pub
L2 structs implementing an api trait must carry the trait name as suffix (e.g. impl Reviewer for DefaultReviewer)

violations.message: 'DefaultSweEdgeRuntimeIsolator' implements 'Validator' but its name does not end with the 'Validator' suffix
violations.fix_hint: Rename 'DefaultSweEdgeRuntimeIsolator' → 'DefaultSweEdgeRuntimeIsolatorValidator'

results.description: saf/mod.rs must exist (SAF facade entry point)
saf/ must not define new pub structs (facade layer only re-exports and wires)
lib.rs must delegate public surface to saf/ via pub use

violations.message: 'src/lib.rs' in project root does not delegate public surface to gateway/ via `pub use`
violations.expected: pub use gateway::*; in lib.rs (gateway/ is the outermost layer)
violations.actual: no `pub use gateway::` or `pub use crate::<gateway>` found
violations.fix_hint: Add `pub use saf::*;` to lib.rs and remove any direct `pub use crate::core` or `pub use crate::api` re-exports

violations.message: 'src/lib.rs' in project root re-exports saf/ directly — when gateway/ exists, only gateway/ should be the public surface
violations.expected: no `pub use saf::` when gateway/ exists
violations.actual: `pub use saf::` found — consumers would bypass gateway/
violations.fix_hint: Add `pub use saf::*;` to lib.rs and remove any direct `pub use crate::core` or `pub use crate::api` re-exports

results.description: lib.rs must not declare core as public (pub mod core is forbidden)
lib.rs must not declare api as public (pub mod api is forbidden — all layers must be private)
L2 peers must not import each other (core/ ↛ spi/, spi/ ↛ core/)
When spi/ exists, lib.rs must declare `mod spi` (private, not pub mod)
L2 must not import from L3 (core/ and spi/ must not depend on saf/)
scripts/ci/ directory exists (if scripts/ present)
scripts/ci/build.sh exists (if scripts/ci/ present)
scripts/ci/test.sh exists (if scripts/ci/ present)
scripts/ci/lint.sh exists (if scripts/ci/ present)
Root contains only allowed entries (whitelist enforcement)

violations.message: Unexpected entry 'test' at feature level (not in feature whitelist)
violations.expected: Only entries from the feature whitelist
violations.fix_hint: Fix the issue detected by builtin check 'root_whitelist'

results.description: All public functions have corresponding tests (name-based)

violations.message: Public function 'is_empty' has no corresponding test
violations.fix_hint: Add a test function like `fn test_is_empty()` or `fn is_empty_works()`

results.description: Tests use @covers: annotation for explicit 1:1 function linking
Trait names must be singular (not plural)
tests/ directory exists at same level as src/
Reports deprecated trait definitions in api/traits.rs for tracking
L2 layers must not define traits (implementations only — traits belong in L1/api)
SEA layer directories must not contain crates (no Cargo.toml inside api/, core/, saf/, spi/)
L2 module filenames must match their primary struct/trait name (with suffix tolerance)
Types in L2 modules must be name-cohesive (SRP enforcement)
Suggest Builder pattern for complex structs (advisory)

violations.message: Struct 'ProfileSpec' has 5 fields (0 optional) - consider Builder pattern
violations.fix_hint: Add 'ProfileSpecBuilder' with fluent setters

Project structure must match declared application_type
Integration tests must not use mocking libraries or mock types
Dependencies used in src/ must have integration or e2e test coverage

results.description: Dependencies declared in Cargo.toml must be used in src/
CLI projects should use dirs/directories crate for XDG-compliant paths
No hardcoded home directory paths in source code
Test files use suffixes consistent with dependency scope (allowing integration-style *_int_test.rs / *_e2e_test.rs in tests/)

mod.rs must contain only module declarations and re-exports — no struct/impl/fn logic

violations.expected: Only module declarations (pub mod) and re-exports (pub use)

violations.message: mod.rs contains logic: function definitions
violations.expected: Only module declarations (pub mod) and re-exports (pub use)
violations.fix_hint: Move logic (structs, impls, functions) out of mod.rs into named files; mod.rs should only have `pub mod` and `pub use` statements

results.description: saf/ must not re-export traits via pub use — wrap trait methods as standalone functions instead
Root must not have both build and bootstrap scripts
src/gateway/ must exist (prerequisite gate for rules 132-133)
gateway/mod.rs must declare pub(crate) mod ingress
gateway/mod.rs must declare pub(crate) mod egress
L2 must not import from L4 (core/ and spi/ must not depend on gateway/)
gateway/ must not import from core/ — gateway/ calls saf/, not core/ directly
saf/ must not import from gateway/ — gateway/ calls saf/, not the reverse
Directory names under src/ must use snake_case
SEA layer files must contain substantive code (not just comments or empty re-exports)
lib.rs must be a real entry point, not a shim with only re-exports
SEA layer mod.rs must not be a crate-level re-export shim
SEA layer directories must meet minimum LOC threshold (api/>10, core/>20, saf/>5)
[lib] and [[bin]] paths must point inside src/
Non-standard [lib] path with src/ present suggests shim directory
Free functions in saf/ or core/ taking an api-defined type as parameter should be methods on that type
Package name must not use SEA layer suffixes as defined in sea_layers config
Workspace root Cargo.toml must not contain [package] — workspace roots must be virtual
Test file prefix must match a source module name, optionally followed by a pub fn name
Weak filesystem patterns — code that produces host-dependent paths (MAIN_SEPARATOR, hardcoded \\, replace-hacks, literal backslashes)
Feature crate must have config/application.toml for runtime configuration
service_type must be defined in project metadata
api/traits.rs must contain the primary trait matching service_type
core/ must implement the primary api trait (impl <PrimaryTrait> for)
Every non-orchestrator crate must have a Validator trait in api/traits.rs
Test functions must not be placeholders (panic!/todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/empty body)
saf/ public function signatures must not name types from core/ (signature leak)
All public types (struct, enum, trait, type alias) must be declared under api/ — no other layer is a legal declaration site
Each pub type/trait in api/ must be in its own standalone file with the file stem matching the snake_case name
lib.rs must contain only module declarations and re-exports — no struct/impl/fn/const definitions
Only one architecture.toml location is allowed: either architecture.toml at root or main/config/architecture.toml, not both
Every top-level directory at the workspace root must be a declared workspace member or an ancestor of one
Config files must map to corresponding ConfigBuilder struct names: <name>.toml → <Name>ConfigBuilder

violations.message: Config file 'default' has no corresponding config type or builder in src/
violations.expected: struct DefaultConfigBuilder or any `DefaultConfig*` reference in src/
violations.actual: no config type reference found in src/
violations.fix_hint: Define `struct DefaultConfigBuilder` with a fluent builder, import a config type (`use dep::DefaultConfig`), or implement a trait (`impl <Trait> for DefaultConfig`)

results.description: Source files must not contain hardcoded secrets, credentials, or API keys
Project must have a security policy file (SECURITY.md, SECURITY.txt, or .github/SECURITY.md)
Every unsafe block must be preceded by a // SAFETY: comment explaining the invariant

all non-mod.rs files in saf/ must be named <last_segment_of_package_name>_svc.rs
Library code must not use println!/eprintln! — use tracing macros (info!, warn!, error!) instead
Source files must not suppress the unused_imports lint via #[allow(unused_imports)]
Cargo dependencies must not use path = "..." — use git or registry sources instead

results.description: SEA layer directory names must not contain underscores — use nested directories instead of underscore-concatenated names
saf/ public function return types depend on crate extensibility: if spi/ exists, return `impl Trait` for downstream polymorphism; if spi/ does not exist, return concrete types only (no trait leaks)

violations.message: saf/ pub fn returns `-> impl SweEdgeRuntimeIsolator` where `SweEdgeRuntimeIsolator` is a local trait — not allowed
violations.expected: Make trait pub(crate) and compose operations inside the facade instead
violations.fix_hint: Change `pub trait SweEdgeRuntimeIsolator` to `pub(crate) trait SweEdgeRuntimeIsolator` and remove this pub fn returning it

violations.message: saf/ pub fn returns `-> impl Validator` where `Validator` is a local trait — not allowed
violations.expected: Make trait pub(crate) and compose operations inside the facade instead
violations.fix_hint: Change `pub trait Validator` to `pub(crate) trait Validator` and remove this pub fn returning it

violations.message: saf/ pub fn returns `-> impl IsolationProfile` but crate has no spi/ directory — must return concrete type
violations.expected: Without spi/, return the concrete impl type, not the trait
violations.fix_hint: Either: (1) Return concrete type instead of `impl IsolationProfile`, or (2) Create src/spi/ to signal polymorphism intent

results.description: api/ layer must not contain `pub struct` outside designated struct homes — flat layout: api/error/, api/types/; domain-grouped layout: api/<domain>/errors/, api/<domain>/types/

violations.message: `pub struct IsolatorConfig` in api/ — traits and type aliases at api/ root; error types in api/error/; value objects in api/types/; implementations in core/
violations.fix_hint: Move to core/ behind an api/ trait; to api/error/ if an error type; or to api/types/ if a value object

violations.message: `pub struct ProfileSpec` in api/ — traits and type aliases at api/ root; error types in api/error/; value objects in api/types/; implementations in core/
violations.fix_hint: Move to core/ behind an api/ trait; to api/error/ if an error type; or to api/types/ if a value object

results.description: Source files must not suppress the dead_code lint via #[allow(dead_code)]
saf/ pub fn return types must be declared in api/types/ (when spi/ is absent)

violations.message: saf/ pub fn returns `IsolationProfileRegistry` which is not declared in api/types/ — concrete return types must come from api/types/
violations.expected: Declare the return type in src/api/types/ and re-export it from saf/
violations.fix_hint: Create src/api/types/isolationprofileregistry.rs with `pub struct/enum IsolationProfileRegistry` and add it to api/types/mod.rs

results.description: saf/ methods must not be thin wrappers — a saf/ method whose entire body delegates to a free function or another method adds no value and signals incomplete consolidation

violations.message: saf/ method `create_swe_edge_runtime_isolator` is a thin wrapper around `crate::core::DefaultSweEdgeRuntimeIsolator::new` — inline the logic or delete the free function
violations.expected: Move the body of the delegated function directly into this method
violations.fix_hint: Copy the body of `crate::core::DefaultSweEdgeRuntimeIsolator::new` into `create_swe_edge_runtime_isolator`, then delete `crate::core::DefaultSweEdgeRuntimeIsolator::new`

violations.message: saf/ method `create_validator` is a thin wrapper around `crate::core::DefaultSweEdgeRuntimeIsolator::new` — inline the logic or delete the free function
violations.expected: Move the body of the delegated function directly into this method
violations.fix_hint: Copy the body of `crate::core::DefaultSweEdgeRuntimeIsolator::new` into `create_validator`, then delete `crate::core::DefaultSweEdgeRuntimeIsolator::new`
